text: Iron treatment has a large effect on improving serum ferritin in iron-deficient non-anaemic endurance athletes.
Iron treatment has a large effect on improving serum iron in iron-deficient non-anaemic endurance athletes.
Iron treatment has a moderate effect on improving VO2max in iron-deficient non-anaemic endurance athletes, even without clinical anaemia.
Iron treatment has a moderate effect on improving haemoglobin concentration in iron-deficient non-anaemic endurance athletes.
Iron treatment duration beyond 80 days shows diminishing returns on serum ferritin improvement, suggesting shorter treatment courses may be more effective per unit time.
specific_value: >80 days reduced effect on serum ferritin

text: Iron deficiency without anaemia is sufficient to impair aerobic capacity in endurance athletes, and correcting it improves VO2max.
